$comment: Strabon Map — machine-readable trust statement (E50). Not the formal Apple PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy spec; an internal-discipline document that asserts what the artifact does and doesn't do. Future feature work that violates any of these claims MUST update this file in the same commit.

networkRequests.$comment: The artifact makes ZERO outbound requests at runtime. All assets are same-origin. Even the optional data/coast.geojson fetch is same-origin. No analytics, no telemetry, no fonts.googleapis.com, no CDN scripts. Adding any outbound request requires updating this list AND amending the landing-screen trust copy (E36).

$comment_thirdParty: There are NO third-party scripts loaded at runtime. The artifact ships with two vendored libraries (vendor/exifr, vendor/libheif), both served from the same origin as static files. Vendored != third-party loaded: see the `vendoredLibraries` section below.

vendoredLibraries.name: exifr
vendoredLibraries.purpose: Reading EXIF GPS + timestamp from HEIC photos during import (E38). The original photo bytes never leave the device.

vendoredLibraries.name: libheif-js
vendoredLibraries.purpose: Decoding HEIC pixel data into a canvas on browsers that don't support HEIC natively (E61). Decoding happens entirely in-process via WASM; no network calls.
vendoredLibraries.loadedWhen: First HEIC photo import in a session

vendoredLibraries.name: @jsquash/jpeg (mozjpeg)
vendoredLibraries.purpose: Encoding keepsake-resolution JPEGs for the Postcard Link share (~25% smaller than the canvas encoder, so more photographs fit in a link on iOS, which cannot encode WebP). Loaded lazily on the first postcard build; photo bytes are processed entirely on-device and only ever leave inside a link the user deliberately sends.

localStorage.purpose: Trip JSON (trip name, stops, dates, journal notes, companion names, theme preference, landing-seen flag). All keys are namespaced under 'strabonMap.*'.

indexedDB.purpose: Photo bytes as JPEG Blobs, keyed by generated photoId (E11). EXIF metadata is stripped during the canvas re-encode before any byte hits the store — the stored photo cannot be reverse-engineered back to a GPS coordinate.

remoteStorage.$comment: There is no backend. Local-first is not a marketing claim, it's a structural property of the artifact: there is no other place the data could go.

permissions.$comment: Browser permissions the artifact may request at runtime.
permissions.$comment_conditional: clipboardWrite may be used by the souvenir export flow (E35) when the user explicitly clicks a 'copy to clipboard' affordance. fileSystemAccess is used only via the standard <input type='file'> upload — not the modern File System Access API.

userRights.deleteAllData: Open browser DevTools > Application > Storage > Clear site data. Removes the IndexedDB photo store and all localStorage trip JSON. The user has full control because the data never left their browser.
userRights.exportAllData: The souvenir export (E35) bundles the active trip as a standalone HTML file. To export every trip, repeat for each one. (E51 — full journal backup — is on the roadmap.)
userRights.rectifyData: Every field is editable in the artifact's UI: trip name, stop name, dates, journal note, companion names, photo set.

complianceContext.$comment: Strabon Map is a personal-use single-file web artifact. The standard frameworks (GDPR, CCPA, COPPA) target processors and controllers of others' data. Strabon Map has none — the user runs it on their own browser against their own data. There is no controller and no processor in the regulatory sense.

auditPolicy.$comment: This file is the source of truth for the trust claims in the README, the landing-screen trust block (E36), and SECURITY.md (E62, planned). Any commit that changes runtime behaviour MUST also update this file when relevant — outbound requests, new vendored deps, new browser permissions, new storage keys.
